repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives signed ego-cluster incidence-polynomial first and joint exposure probabilities; closest pid_network_exposure_v1 is a partial-ID joint-completion LP problem, not probability algebra for design-based HT weights.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 gives the general exposure-mapping/IPW architecture and exposure-probability inputs (https://arxiv.org/abs/1305.6156); UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013 gives exact probabilities for selected graph-cluster exposure conditions (https://arxiv.org/abs/1305.6979), but neither states the signed-polynomial inclusion-exclusion formula.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Experimentation/Stat entry derives an ego-overlap dependency graph for HT exposure contrasts; panel_approx_nbhd_interference_v1 is a failed comparator-frontier proposal, not this primitive overlap bound.

published_prior_art.cite: Chin2018 assumes or works through a restricted dependency graph for interference CLTs (https://arxiv.org/abs/1804.03105); the new part is deriving Delta_n <= 2 q_n kappa_n (omega_n - 1) from ego exposure supports.

repo_prior_art.cite: No accepted catalogue item contains the fixed-potential ego-cluster HT oracle Wald theorem with exact signed-incidence exposure weights.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 proves exposure-mapping HT asymptotics under local dependence and Chin2018 proves Stein CLTs under restricted dependency degree; LiuHuZhang2026 is closest on ego-cluster designs but is model-based rather than fixed-potential HT design-based inference (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00534).

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives a sharp conservative design-based variance envelope for nonadditive ego-cluster HT exposure contrasts.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017, ChattopadhyayImaiZubizarreta2023 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.03268), KungSussman2022 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2209.01053), Leung2019ANI (https://arxiv.org/abs/1911.07085), and GaoDing2023 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.07476) are adjacent, but do not solve the stated sharp nonadditive ego-cluster envelope.

repo_prior_art.cite: Standard sanity reduction to local-dependence exposure-map benchmarks.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 and Chin2018 already cover the bounded-degree dependency-graph HT benchmark.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:ego-overlap-clt
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Wald interval is oracle because it uses sigma_n rather than an observed conservative variance estimator.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The theorem is only an oracle design-based CLT/coverage benchmark under the stated primitive overlap, positivity, boundedness, and nondegenerate-variance conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:primitive-degree-rate
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The CLT is conditional on the primitive overlap frontier 2 q_n kappa_n (omega_n - 1) = o(n^(1/4)); it does not claim validity for arbitrary ego-cluster reuse.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline CLT is bound to the bounded-overlap sparse-ego regime.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:conservative-variance
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The computable conservative variance envelope is explicitly open and no operational non-oracle confidence interval is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Non-oracle coverage is only a solve target until Vbar_n and its sharpness/nontriviality properties are constructed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make the variance-envelope handle a fully explicit finite-dimensional optimization problem: variables, dimension, bounded-potential constraints, observed-outcome consistency constraints, exact pi/pi_ij inputs, and minimality criterion for sharpness.
Add a corollary that recovers the Aronow-Samii/Chin local-dependence benchmark in the singleton-literal case and then lists a concrete signed-polynomial exposure rule not covered by the benchmark.
If the variance OEQ succeeds, add a feasible Wald theorem using Vbar_n and separate pointwise conservativeness from uniform conservativeness over the CLT-qualified class.
Add Lu-Shi-Ding 2026 randomized-saturation spillover work as an adjacent design comparator only if the prose keeps it clearly separate from ego-cluster fixed-exposure HT inference.

recommended_next_step: Advance this angle to Stage 0: the revised proposal is field-tier, novel on the repo and published axes, and the remaining limitations are accepted scope rather than blocking defects.
